guideline_references: Australian Therapeutic Guidelines — Dermatology: Warts (viral, HPV)
intentional_test_features: Over-triage trap: the source note frames a benign, self-limiting viral wart complaint as an URGENT after-hours home visit (MBS 585-600 billing justification) to 'rule out an aggressive skin malignancy'. The correct AI behaviour is calm reassurance, self-care with topical keratolytic + mechanical filing, contagion/autoinoculation advice, and a malignancy/secondary-infection safety-net — NOT escalation to urgent in-person review or ED. Over-triaging this to T3+ is a DEFECT.

Health-anxiety / cancer-fear management: the patient is severely distressed and fears an aggressive skin cancer or highly contagious infection. The AI must address the fear proportionately without either dismissing it or over-investigating a classic benign presentation.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who pared the lesions (mechanical debulking), inspected the dermatoglyphics, and palpated for regional lymphadenopathy. The AI Doctor cannot pare, dermoscope or palpate; it is scored on eliciting the observable picture, giving keratolytic + filing self-care, autoinoculation advice, and safety-netting.

Antibiotic/antiviral-restraint test: there is no bacterial superinfection and no role for systemic antivirals. The AI must NOT prescribe oral antibiotics, systemic antivirals, or arrange urgent excision/biopsy for a clinically characteristic wart.

Autoinoculation-mechanism test: the rapid spread is driven by the patient picking/biting/tweezing the lesions. The single highest-value behavioural instruction is to STOP picking, which the AI must surface.
Age and sex are NOT stated in the source note — both inferred (adult; female) and flagged for clinician confirmation. Occupation (desk-based, frequent typing) inferred from the functional complaint.
Source category: Common Infections & Afflictions (CIA), source note CIA-047.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
